name: Bill Weld
subject: Re: Coronavirus
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Weld for President 2020 Presidential Campaign Committee · United States
body:
|
---
Hi Friend — As our nation responds to the coronavirus and its impact across our communities, I wanted to make sure to put Tuesday’s email back at the top of your inbox. Now more than ever, it’s important that we continue to look out for our families and neighbors, that we follow the directives of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, and that we stay vigilant in our day-to-day activities. Likewise, all across the nation, state and local officials, school districts and health departments are taking steps they believe are necessary to reduce impacts and risks at the local level. It's critical that we all pay attention and cooperate. We’ll get through this crisis by working together. \- Bill \---------- Forwarded message \--------- Hey Friend — Today, I want to talk about the coronavirus. With newly confirmed cases occurring throughout the nation, it’s important that we all have the most up to date information. Our state and our local governments are working together with the NIH, the CDC, and hospitals across the country to contain the virus and, if needed, to make sure that people have access to diagnostic tests and treatment. **But it’s extremely important that we continue to listen to the public health community and follow proper hygiene protocols and procedures.** We all have a responsibility to help contain the virus. That means that there are simple steps each of us should be doing, including:
* Washing your hands regularly (especially before eating, or after coughing or sneezing),
* Staying home if you are sick, and
* Disinfecting frequently touched objects with a household cleaning spray.

If you experience symptoms of the coronavirus, **contact your primary care physician and your local County Department of Health immediately**. Additionally, you can visit the dedicated Coronavirus website at the Centers for Disease Control for the latest updates at [https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/index.html](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=7Ho65PobbrrlpD_Ow9Pq_3y6Rl1ZYx2Sap7xXzPRZTDO8zxbFTk3DJ9v9IOTAkfAFSqsl_Rqm6r4ZFDZyeREvw&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20200312_engs&n=1). I want to reiterate that while we all feel anxious and overwhelmed by both the virus and the news surrounding it, the threat to the general public remains low -- just as long as we all stay vigilant, and we do our part to follow safe virus prevention practices. We’ll do everything we can to keep you up to date on the latest developments. Thanks, Bill Weld
Paid for by Weld for President 2020 Presidential Campaign Committee · United States
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](dpwillis67@gmail.com). To stop receiving emails, [click here](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/unsubscribe?e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20200312_engs&n=2).
You can also keep up with Team Weld on [Twitter](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=7Sn_jlQ3J2wHxMyMtp8UNEee0J3CzZBj8v0TKy2tYMM&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20200312_engs&n=3) or [Facebook](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6sD5mbawGI5P2loKSMnHaUPJQdagn1SdGMQUy6UvfZqO&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20200312_engs&n=4).
Created with [NationBuilder](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=SWqLExi-4iBlEOa5qgO58jA2d62deOsOLwOgchKDecY&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20200312_engs&n=5), software for leaders.

We’ll do everything we can to keep you up to date on the latest developments. Thanks, Bill Weld Paid for by Weld for President 2020 Presidential Campaign Committee · United States

name: Reverend Warnock
subject: Friend, this is important
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
Let me explain why your support is so critical - and what will happen if we fall short.
| | |
---
Friend: In a moment, I'm going to ask you to become one of the 1,000 Founding Members we need in our fight to flip Georgia blue and take back the Senate for Democrats. **But first, let me explain why your support is so important - and what will happen if we fall short.**
I wasn't born with a silver spoon in my mouth. I had 11 brothers and sisters and I grew up in public housing on the west side of Savannah, Georgia. But, from there, I went on to earn a college degree, and worked my way up to serving as the senior pastor at Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.'s church. Now, I have the chance to become the first black senator this state has _ever_ elected.
But the truth is, the opportunities that made my journey possible - from Kayton Homes to Dr. King's pulpit - are growing further and further out of reach for too many people across this country. I'm running for Senate to change that, but I can't win without help of grassroots supporters like you.
**That 's why I'm asking: [friend, will you chip in before midnight tomorrow to become a Founding Member of our campaign - so we can flip Georgia blue, take back the Senate for Democrats and build a country in which _everyone_ , no matter who you are or where you come from, has a shot at the American dream?](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.%3D%3D/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warnock-for-georgia_email?refcode=jtk105-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")**
[](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.%3D%3D/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warnock-for-georgia_email?refcode=jtk105-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")
The stakes have never been higher: Nate Silver's FiveThirtyEight confirms that Democrats can flip the Senate - and analysts at _The Washington Post_ say that winning in Georgia is key to making it happen.
Democrats cannot take back the Senate if we lose this critical battleground state. Plain and simple. That's why Mitch McConnell and the GOP are zeroing in on our race - and why my Republican opponent has already started spending $20 million of her own money against me.
**So please, friend, this is important:[If you care about taking back the Senate for Democrats, I'm counting on you to rush $5 or more right now to become one of the 1,000 essential Founding Members we need by midnight tomorrow to power forward our fight to flip Georgia blue!](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.%3D%3D/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warnock-for-georgia_email?refcode=jtk105-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")**
Thank you,

Reverend Warnock

name: James Mackler
subject: This isn't a partisan issue.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James Mackler for U
body:
Not when the health of Americans is on the line.
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa1a3/18d6a294/4ccee13d/26d61843/1338053662/VEsH/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | When there are threats to our public health and safety, we need our leaders to take action with a swift and robust response. But right now, Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell is stalling on critical legislation that would provide: \- Free coronavirus testing
\- Emergency medical leave
\- Extended unemployment insurance
\- Food assistance
\- Critical health care funding [_**Tell McConnell to bring up emergency coronavirus legislation for a vote now ->**_](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa1a3/18d6a294/4ccee13d/26d61840/1338053662/VEsE/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e200312p") McConnell called the legislation the Democrats' "wish list," but it's precisely the opposite -- _it 's a list of needs._ This isn't a partisan issue -- not when the health and well-being of Americans is on the line. No one should have to choose between taking measures to stay healthy or putting themselves and others at risk by going to work. In moments like this, we need our leaders to step up and work together to get things done. [_**Will you add your name and call on McConnell to take action on emergency coronavirus legislation?**_](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa1a3/18d6a294/4ccee13d/26d61841/1338053662/VEsF/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "http://secure.jamesmackler.com/e200312p") In service, James

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Coronavirus Update: Keeping Hoosiers Safe
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
We’re working hard to make sure Indiana is prepared to keep Hoosiers healthy during the current coronavirus outbreak. | | |
---|---|---
Friend,

Governor Holcomb and Indiana’s healthcare professionals are working hard to keep Hoosiers healthy during the current coronavirus outbreak.

That’s why Indiana is taking actions now that will reduce the spread of the virus, including:

* Limiting non-essential gatherings to fewer than 250 people
* Granting schools a 20-day waiver of the requirement for 180 instructional days
* Instituting social distancing at child care and adult care facilities
* Restricting and screening visitors at nursing facilities and hospitals
* Suspending visitation at the Indiana Department of Correction
* Limiting public exposure for older adults and those with known underlying health issues
* Encouraging businesses to utilize telework policies, if possible

We want to make sure you have access to the latest public health resources that contain accurate and up-to-date information. The Indiana State Department of Health’s website has a dashboard tracking coronavirus cases statewide, and the department’s call center is staffed 24/7 to answer your questions (call 317-233-7125 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. or 317-233-1325 after 8 p.m.).

Below are additional resources to find best practices and the latest information on the virus:
**Indiana State Department of Health Coronavirus Overview: **
[https://www.in.gov/isdh/28470.htm](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=c03f8d41bd8e442281b3f5c7184aa109&_e=L4-whz0Ycxj8VymATNrST_aloGZ4wk2oeTUcVMh4FsxMHFhx-tSAJFL5hX96GUZEvGnj8JRu5QxJdC_zIToQ6wQooT1BJCTzuQdJhw9ul0eGyjy3oq0zEMUDhU7Ere-fRzZ6E45pvKhJn7M4I5WkPhgACYlZMSIE-2mHKs_mZU92IqAeegnxT2UxLS7z0LxvcG-Cp-vY3iFsyHI6OcH8Fw8kz-0ToWL7QhEcwf9dilNdLcvTu8R9BwkY98v3FnYI_dGYPdjT25_-sY86MPw4HQ%3D%3D)

**Centers for Disease Control and Prevention:**
[https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/about/index.html](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=c03f8d41bd8e442281b3f5c7184aa109&_e=L4-whz0Ycxj8VymATNrSTyeY3T5Bltb6pEgIqCZbMm4X7tTTG1TokGvtCDLEQwjml3fg2sm3Dwmz7rdPOrq98WODm6m6_zDv5Evdju-n_CjEo6C2R5YfiZ5Zp1khe0Abdpu6DELIJdw1Sp-cqFPf8C-TIUA20A3fa6CcV5vT2Sk6f16qh1Vzs7ER4rxQBUthiv5Wey1gXfirm0227S7fRN2XBirs39k_NJRh10EejyEf0Y8Wxmd-XJCpBT1ZTL5Atln0yIYK2AL71en26jhoEWWnOXiphO6Kb64OHfcXSlCluncAIQ0SPhvb5fAvd7ad)

**World Health Organization Advice for the Public:** [https://www.who.int/emergencies/diseases/novel-coronavirus-2019/advice-for-public](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=c03f8d41bd8e442281b3f5c7184aa109&_e=L4-whz0Ycxj8VymATNrST4DcM22hGZ-D6U_KOsiQe9uUBV6vC01cduC8n5gOZJC3bpgjtJ_S_87kUyk-6JEdr24RYjA0IyRiVDJ308vQR19Hu-Jxts9p7qB_JpUW7lFL-31ZyconS6VeQDU2g0v3quCJgUODC6dk3YGZMA-XDovWC27WmTTSCJYc-VradupzTB91G6IUh_aERpItX2V9lev-5FoOVE9PbDkQ5cSCWn8ZmU0PmaTTaMxZpy37ITHNYpCE_8XBESn8uqiDVOCJfQIHbm5nqWc8LFusBT5Vh8BzE-rqpgR3DjZV505DCpA2Y-gKeJxdSodv3qo7pxv8Lg%3D%3D)

**World Health Organization Q &A;:**
[https://www.who.int/news-room/q-a-detail/q-a-coronaviruses](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=c03f8d41bd8e442281b3f5c7184aa109&_e=L4-whz0Ycxj8VymATNrST4ribP-Ez-Hbvin6Ywr7b0bLs3ijFpbUOwodzuJ-fvz0NNGaTib1QgQsMtIaouAEao5JU0wXdvMAs4IggWDr11xRVyUA5kthYCXwzH_ghNhyozCqZ21R8WfU10199yW9h9r6OUXkpvJcebrt4BeE8QtUPuGm6_29emVXEkWdVIZp8Z-L1M9hOIXbkFhWiEvzWpsbIiqqIB_Gulj-9z-x_fAaPdQpW-xlDZyyUsLoIzCz_pFQS1_K1IdSkdbFb-7HFt9LQyyXJZ-LpfUamLI7BIgisbu_gQhPJPEHbOukFg7m)

**World Health Organization Coronavirus Overview:**
[https://www.who.int/health-topics/coronavirus](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=c03f8d41bd8e442281b3f5c7184aa109&_e=L4-whz0Ycxj8VymATNrSTwSiBm1y_WUk2DoXj08LeLwnwi0RcY-lVqp4_TcPsINuOVGtPCMVtJDw5KHdxg7c_kF2WTH5r_1VaQE049KUtJn-u4E9eJyLvBvu5LT9zwtCdxOxsIyPJ8eUJA4yCMbQxo6XN6vNHo4KlUGeUBIwyI8-wr6wC1tkZjDtq5TP1Q8V5pvBrom7Z2v0UtkCaXpCV7RN-8y_0UtPwf62GqNTLaUhTVz64hkazWUcX5oz_HwLQBdLsya2A_m2_xBsE12mIQhj5pKqp8ienxQ0LUXH8h4%3D)

Just as he has since the start of this outbreak, Governor Holcomb is working directly with our partners at all levels with one goal -- **keeping Hoosiers safe.**
-Holcomb Crew
